location.office_time_required: flexible in terms of time spent remote and in-office with the ability to travel as needed

level_signals: 2+ years in a sales role focused on building relationships and closing deals
1+ years of experience in Outbound Sales
Reports to an Inside Sales Manager

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 2+ years in a sales role focused on building relationships and closing deals
You have 1+ years of experience in Outbound Sales being held to DTT minimums
You are creative, motivated, and passionate about sales and have a dynamic personality
You are comfortable working in a scaling startup environment and adapting quickly
You value collaboration and find opportunities within change
You are tech-savvy and navigate sales and internal tools quickly (Salesforce, Outreach, Sales Navigator, Google Apps)

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have experience working with Franchise accounts (nice to have)

scope.surface: Inside Account Development — SMB merchant upsell (Ads, Loyalty & Promotions)
scope.business_model: B2B2C marketplace; upselling advertising and promotional products to existing restaurant/merchant partners
primary_metrics_inferred: Volume of outbound calls and emails (DTT minimums)
Merchant partner activation of incremental products
Retention and satisfaction of SMB partner base
core_responsibilities: Upsell incremental products and services (Ads, Loyalty & Promotions) to existing merchants
Make a high volume of calls and emails to existing partners daily
Collaborate with Inside Account Management Team to achieve goals of Managed Partners
Activate additional products on the DoorDash platform to empower merchant partner growth
Cultivate unique solutions through data and analytical tools
Use email marketing automation tools to creatively engage partners

green_flags: Clearly defined product focus (Ads, Loyalty & Promotions) — not a vague generalist role
Strong team culture signals (collaboration, partner-first mindset)
DoorDash is a large, established platform with significant SMB merchant base providing large addressable market for upsell
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed anywhere in the JD
DTT minimums mentioned but not defined — unclear performance bar
High-volume outbound calling role may have significant quota pressure not fully described

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required + reporting structure)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and company context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'flexible in terms of time spent remote and in-office' language)
ambiguities: Location type: JD says team is 'predominantly located in Phoenix, AZ' but also says the role is 'flexible in terms of time spent remote and in-office' — could be interpreted as hybrid with Phoenix base or partially remote-eligible. Treated as hybrid with Phoenix as primary city.
DTT minimums mentioned as a hard metric ('being held to DTT minimums') but 'DTT' is never defined in the JD — likely an internal DoorDash metric acronym.

'You are creative, motivated, and passionate about sales' appears under 'We're excited about you because…' which is the required qualifications section, so treated as hard; however it reads as cultural fit language.
